description: The scope of work includes the demolition and reconstruction of fire damaged portion of the one-story pre-engineered metal storage building as indicated on the drawings, including: a. Remove and replacement of prefinished metal roof and wall panels. b. Replacement of cold formed steel roof purlins, channel columns and miscellaneous framing channels. c. Hollow metal frames and doors, including storage locker coiling overhead doors and corrugated metal swing doors. d. Replacement of vinyl faced batt insulation at underside of roof system. e. Replacement of electrical distribution panels, conduits, conductors, lighting, power and special systems. f. Replacement of mechanical split systems and ductwork.

description: A: Work consist of tearing down what is left of damage brick fence and rebuilding it back to its original state. Brick fence measurements are 45 ft long 7.5 ft tall two brick thick with three brick columns 2 ft wide 7.5 ft tall. After tearing down brick fence and cleaning brick we will lose 20% of brick on the brick fence the cost of extra brick will included in estimate and all debris will be haul away. B: Work consist of installing new concrete foundation if needed. Foundation mersurements are 18” by 18” 45 ft long with number 5 steel rebar installed and columns foundation 2ft by 2ft with number 5 steel rebar installed.. Price includes all labor and materials. C:Barbed Wire Removal: VCC guidelines do not allow for barbed wire and will not be reinstallation as part of the rebuilding of the masonry wall.
